hypotheses.title: Mn Speciation as the Missing Variable in Manganese Neurotoxicity: A Unifying Framework
key_issues: Core framework (speciation determines toxicity) published since 2006 in multiple papers
Globus pallidus lowest buffering capacity claim unverified - literature cites DAT transport, glutamate, metabolic rate instead
Deinococcus bridge is analogical, not mechanistic - bacterial mM Mn ≠ mammalian µM Mn
Extension of known work, not novel discovery

hypotheses.title: Compartment-Specific Mn-OP Formation in Mitochondria Explains Protective vs Toxic Mn Pools
key_issues: Quantitatively impossible: 0.7% complex formation at inflated 10 mM Pi; actual Pi is 1-5 mM
Brain Mn 3-10 µM vs experimental 100+ µM - 10-30x concentration mismatch
Ca2+ and Mg2+ at mM levels outcompete Mn2+ for Pi coordination (Irving-Williams)
MnSOD already explains mitochondrial Mn protection without invoking Mn-OP

hypotheses.title: Deinococcus DP1 Motif Identifies Cryptic Mn-Coordinating Sequence in Human Neuroprotective Proteins
key_issues: DEH motif is ubiquitous - D, E, H among most abundant amino acids, thousands of proteome hits expected
DEH binds Cu2+ as well or better than Mn2+ (Irving-Williams series)
More research proposal than testable hypothesis
Base rate neglect: finding DEH in brain proteins is expected by chance

hypotheses.title: Mn-OP Formulation Potentiates Existing MnSOD Mimetics (MnTE-2-PyP) by Preventing Mn Redistribution Toxicity
key_issues: Core premise directly contradicted: Gad 2013 (PMID 23704100) shows no measurable free Mn released from MnTE-2-PyP
Mn(III) porphyrins designed to not lose Mn center
BMX-001 in Phase II trials with no Mn toxicity signal
Hypothesis solves a non-existent problem
hypotheses.kill_reason: Core premise (porphyrin degradation releases free Mn2+) directly contradicted by published nonclinical safety data (PMID 23704100). The drug is metabolically stable with no measurable free Mn release.

hypotheses.title: EPR-Detectable Free Mn2+ Fraction as Diagnostic Biomarker for Mn Neurotoxicity Risk
key_issues: Blood Mn is ~70-275 nM, typical EPR sensitivity is µM range - 10-100x gap
MRI T1 hyperintensity already serves as practical Mn accumulation biomarker
MnRBCs emerging as competing novel biomarker with simpler methodology
Clinical EPR translation faces major regulatory and equipment barriers

hypotheses.title: Mn Speciation-Dependent Ferroptosis Sensitivity: Free Mn2+ Promotes While Mn-OP Inhibits
key_issues: Mn-ferroptosis connection already published in 5+ papers (2022-2024)
Mn2+ Fenton chemistry is ineffective - Mn is a poor Fenton catalyst vs Fe2+
Actual Mn-ferroptosis mechanism is via iron overload and ferritinophagy, not Mn Fenton
Deinococcus lipid peroxidation resistance also from carotenoids, not just Mn-OP
hypotheses.kill_reason: Double kill: (1) Mn-ferroptosis is published and active research area with 5+ papers; (2) Proposed mechanism (Mn2+ Fenton chemistry) is wrong — Mn2+ is a poor Fenton catalyst. Actual mechanism is iron overload-mediated.

critic_questions.question: What specific mechanism does the Deinococcus model add that was not already in the Michalke (2016) speciation-neurodegeneration review?
At 0.7% complex formation (your own calculation), how does Mn-OP explain mitochondrial Mn protection when MnSOD already provides a complete explanation?
What evidence supports MnTE-2-PyP releasing free Mn upon degradation? Gad 2013 safety study finds no measurable free Mn release.
How does Mn2+ do Fenton chemistry when published data shows it is an ineffective Fenton catalyst? Actual Mn-ferroptosis mechanism is via iron overload.
